title: How to include multiple hostAliases in yml file?
body:
### Discussed in https://github.com/containers/podman/discussions/15386
Originally posted by **NeilDavisNPS** August 19, 2022 Hello, I have the following snippet in a yml file: ``` hostAliases: - ip: "10.0.1.74" hostnames: - "mysql" - ip: "10.0.1.150" hostames: - "my.server.com" ``` When I run ``` podman play kube compose.yml ``` the resulting container's host file is populated with only the first alias (mysql). Is this expected? Here's the podman version info ```podman version Client: Podman Engine Version: 4.1.1 API Version: 4.1.1 Go Version: go1.17.12 Built: Thu Aug 4 00:36:19 2022 OS/Arch: linux/amd64``` Cheers.

comments.body:
This looks like a bug.
@umohnani8 PTAL

I tested this on podman v4.2 + upstream podman and the issue has been fixed in the latest version.

Here is the yaml I tested with 
```
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: hostaliases-pod
spec:
  restartPolicy: Never
  hostAliases:
  - ip: "127.0.0.1"
    hostnames:
    - "foo.local"
    - "bar.local"
  - ip: "10.1.2.3"
    hostnames:
    - "foo.remote"
    - "bar.remote"
  containers:
  - name: cat-hosts
    image: alpine
    command:
    - sleep
    - "1000"
```

With podman play kube, the /etc/hosts file in the container has both hostAlias entries from the kube yaml

```
➜  podman git:(4.2) ✗ ./bin/podman play kube host-alias.yaml 
Pod:
72eec3da4558b26b120a31c970b3552122b0d18341c4e2590e1cef2f5988c352
Container:
d8ccd6780774823a02b5c595c77884c885e1b1aff7ace736e41006d3dd2d7a8e

➜  podman git:(4.2) ✗ podman ps
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE                                    COMMAND     CREATED        STATUS            PORTS       NAMES
2f8074e0f4c9  localhost/podman-pause:4.2.0-1661397588              4 seconds ago  Up 4 seconds ago              72eec3da4558-infra
d8ccd6780774  docker.io/library/alpine:latest                      3 seconds ago  Up 4 seconds ago              hostaliases-pod-cat-hosts
➜  podman git:(4.2) ✗ ./bin/podman exec -it d8cc sh
/ # cat /etc/hosts
127.0.0.1       foo.local
127.0.0.1       bar.local
10.1.2.3        foo.remote
10.1.2.3        bar.remote
...

✗ ./bin/podman version
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.2.0
API Version:  4.2.0
Go Version:   go1.16.15
Git Commit:   7fe5a419cfd2880df2028ad3d7fd9378a88a04f4
Built:        Wed Aug 24 23:19:48 2022
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

Are there any other entries that are expected?

> I tested this on podman v4.2 + upstream podman and the issue has been fixed in the latest version.
> 
> Here is the yaml I tested with
> 
> ```
> apiVersion: v1
> kind: Pod
> metadata:
>   name: hostaliases-pod
> spec:
>   restartPolicy: Never
>   hostAliases:
>   - ip: "127.0.0.1"
>     hostnames:
>     - "foo.local"
>     - "bar.local"
>   - ip: "10.1.2.3"
>     hostnames:
>     - "foo.remote"
>     - "bar.remote"
>   containers:
>   - name: cat-hosts
>     image: alpine
>     command:
>     - sleep
>     - "1000"
> ```
> 
> With podman play kube, the /etc/hosts file in the container has both hostAlias entries from the kube yaml
> 
> ```
> ➜  podman git:(4.2) ✗ ./bin/podman play kube host-alias.yaml 
> Pod:
> 72eec3da4558b26b120a31c970b3552122b0d18341c4e2590e1cef2f5988c352
> Container:
> d8ccd6780774823a02b5c595c77884c885e1b1aff7ace736e41006d3dd2d7a8e
> 
> ➜  podman git:(4.2) ✗ podman ps
> CONTAINER ID  IMAGE                                    COMMAND     CREATED        STATUS            PORTS       NAMES
> 2f8074e0f4c9  localhost/podman-pause:4.2.0-1661397588              4 seconds ago  Up 4 seconds ago              72eec3da4558-infra
> d8ccd6780774  docker.io/library/alpine:latest                      3 seconds ago  Up 4 seconds ago              hostaliases-pod-cat-hosts
> ➜  podman git:(4.2) ✗ ./bin/podman exec -it d8cc sh
> / # cat /etc/hosts
> 127.0.0.1       foo.local
> 127.0.0.1       bar.local
> 10.1.2.3        foo.remote
> 10.1.2.3        bar.remote
> ...
> 
> ✗ ./bin/podman version
> Client:       Podman Engine
> Version:      4.2.0
> API Version:  4.2.0
> Go Version:   go1.16.15
> Git Commit:   7fe5a419cfd2880df2028ad3d7fd9378a88a04f4
> Built:        Wed Aug 24 23:19:48 2022
> OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
> ```
> 
> Are there any other entries that are expected?

Hello, 

if all the hostAliases are added to the container's /etc/hosts file, then that is all that I expect.
This requires an upgrade from podman engine 4.1.1 to podman engine 4.2.0, is that correct?

Thanks.
Yes, please upgrade to podman 4.2 and test it out. I am going to close this issue for now, but please reopen or open another one if you run into any other issues.
